rawText:
The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has removed roughly a fifth of global crude supply from the market, sending oil prices surging past $119 per barrel. This disruption, triggered by the American-Israeli military campaign against Iran, now ranks among the most severe supply interruptions in the modern era. International agencies have urged measures as drastic as working from home to manage demand. Yet the consequences of this single chokepoint shutting down are playing out very differently depending on which side of the Atlantic you stand on.

In the United States, the shale revolution of the past decade has transformed the country into a net energy exporter, a dramatic reversal from its import-dependent past. This structural shift provides a genuine cushion, but it is far from a complete shield. America still imports petroleum equivalent to over 40 percent of what it consumes domestically, and gasoline prices have climbed by roughly a third in just one month. The economic pain falls unevenly: low-income households spend nearly double the share of their budgets on fuel and electricity compared to wealthy ones. Meanwhile, energy companies and oil-producing states such as Texas are reaping windfall profits. The result is a stark internal redistribution of wealth flowing from struggling consumers to shareholders, deepening an inequality that was already politically toxic before the crisis began.

Europe faces an even harsher reckoning. Natural gas prices on the continent have climbed back above levels that recall the turmoil following the 2022 disruption of Russian supplies, and they remain roughly five times higher than in the United States. For energy-intensive industries like chemicals and metals manufacturing, which form a significant part of the European economy, these costs are existential. In Germany's basic chemicals sector, energy already accounts for more than 40 percent of value added, leaving little margin to absorb further price spikes. Governments across the continent are scrambling with familiar tools: limiting how often fuel stations can raise prices, freezing costs at the pump, and debating windfall taxes.

The deeper European challenge, however, is structural. The continent is in the middle of an enormously expensive transition from fossil fuels to renewables, requiring an estimated 1.4 trillion euros in grid investment by 2040. Countries with existing advantages in nuclear or hydroelectric power resist sharing infrastructure with neighbors still dependent on gas. The carbon pricing system that underpins climate policy is under political attack from leaders desperate to lower industrial costs. Every stakeholder, from consumers to corporations to national governments, is trying to push the burden of this transformation onto someone else.

What unites both sides of the Atlantic is a sobering lesson: even net energy exporters cannot fully escape a global price shock, and no amount of domestic production eliminates vulnerability to disruptions in a deeply interconnected market. The crisis has exposed how fragile energy security remains and how unevenly the costs of that fragility are distributed within societies. For policymakers everywhere, the question is no longer whether to diversify energy sources but how quickly they can do so before the next disruption arrives.

overview.title: How an Energy Shock Ripples Differently Across Continents

sentences.original: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has removed roughly a fifth of global crude supply from the market, sending oil prices surging past $119 per barrel.
sentences.translation: 호르무즈 해협의 봉쇄로 전 세계 원유 공급의 약 5분의 1이 시장에서 사라졌으며, 유가는 배럴당 119달러를 돌파하며 급등했다.

grammarElements.text: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: roughly a fifth of global crude supply
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: sending oil prices surging past $119 per barrel
grammarElements.label: 결과 분사구문

vocabulary.example: The closure of the factory left hundreds of workers unemployed.
Global crude prices have been volatile since the conflict began.
Demand for electric vehicles is surging across Europe.

expressions.usage: 분수 표현은 영어 저널리즘에서 백분율 대신 자주 사용되어 직관적 충격을 전달한다

patterns.pattern: [Event] has [past participle] [quantity], sending [consequence]
patterns.explanation: 하나의 사건이 정량적 영향을 미친 후 그 결과를 분사구문으로 이어붙이는 뉴스 리드 패턴
examples: The earthquake has destroyed thousands of homes, sending rescue teams scrambling across the region.
The ban has removed a quarter of imports from the supply chain, sending prices spiraling upward.

drillQuestions.question: The closure has removed roughly a ______ of global crude supply from the market.

sentences.notes: 기사의 리드 문장으로, 핵심 사건(봉쇄)과 그 정량적 영향(5분의 1), 결과(유가 급등)를 한 문장에 압축한다. sending 분사구문이 인과관계를 자연스럽게 연결.

koreanAlignment.en: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz

sentences.original: This disruption, triggered by the American-Israeli military campaign against Iran, now ranks among the most severe supply interruptions in the modern era.
sentences.translation: 미국-이스라엘의 대이란 군사 작전이 촉발한 이 공급 교란은 현재 근대사에서 가장 심각한 공급 중단 사태 중 하나로 꼽힌다.

grammarElements.text: triggered by the American-Israeli military campaign against Iran
grammarElements.label: 과거분사 삽입구 (원인)

grammarElements.text: among the most severe supply interruptions in the modern era
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 보어

vocabulary.example: Supply chain disruptions have driven up costs for manufacturers worldwide.
This hurricane ranks among the deadliest natural disasters in the region's history.

expressions.usage: 삽입구로 사건의 원인을 간결하게 밝히는 저널리즘 표현

patterns.pattern: [Event], triggered by [cause], now ranks among the most [adjective] [category]
patterns.explanation: 사건을 제시하고 삽입구로 원인을 밝힌 뒤 역사적 맥락에서의 심각성을 평가하는 구조
examples: This recession, triggered by the banking collapse, now ranks among the most prolonged downturns in decades.
This spill, triggered by a pipeline rupture, now ranks among the worst environmental disasters on record.

drillQuestions.question: This disruption now ______ among the most severe supply interruptions.

sentences.notes: triggered by 삽입구가 주어와 동사 사이에 놓여 원인 정보를 압축적으로 전달한다. ranks among은 역사적 비교를 통해 심각성을 강조하는 핵심 표현.

koreanAlignment.en: triggered by the American-Israeli military campaign

sentences.original: International agencies have urged measures as drastic as working from home to manage demand.
sentences.translation: 국제기구들은 수요 관리를 위해 재택근무만큼이나 극단적인 조치를 촉구했다.

grammarElements.text: measures as drastic as working from home
grammarElements.label: as ... as 비교구문을 포함한 목적어

vocabulary.example: Health officials urged residents to evacuate before the storm arrived.
Drastic spending cuts were needed to avoid a government shutdown.

expressions.usage: 비교 대상이 예상 밖일 때 충격 효과를 주는 동등비교 구문

patterns.pattern: [Authority] has urged measures as [adjective] as [surprising example] to [purpose]
patterns.explanation: 권위 있는 기관의 권고를 소개하되, 비교 대상의 뜻밖임을 통해 위기의 심각성을 부각하는 패턴
examples: The WHO has urged measures as extreme as travel bans to contain the outbreak.
Regulators have urged measures as radical as price caps to stabilize the market.

drillQuestions.question: International agencies have urged measures as ______ as working from home.

sentences.notes: as drastic as working from home이라는 비교가 독자에게 위기의 체감 수준을 전달한다. 재택근무가 팬데믹의 상징이기에 그에 필적하는 위기라는 함의가 담겨 있다.

koreanAlignment.en: as drastic as working from home

sentences.original: Yet the consequences of this single chokepoint shutting down are playing out very differently depending on which side of the Atlantic you stand on.
sentences.translation: 그러나 이 단일 병목 지점의 폐쇄가 가져온 결과는 대서양의 어느 쪽에 서 있느냐에 따라 매우 다르게 전개되고 있다.

grammarElements.text: the consequences of this single chokepoint shutting down
grammarElements.label: 동명사를 포함한 주어

grammarElements.text: depending on which side of the Atlantic you stand on
grammarElements.label: 분사구문 (조건)

vocabulary.example: The Suez Canal is one of the world's most critical maritime chokepoints.
The political drama is playing out in real time across social media.

expressions.phrase: depending on which side of the Atlantic
expressions.usage: 미국과 유럽의 차이를 지리적 은유로 표현하는 관용적 구문

patterns.pattern: Yet the consequences of [event] are playing out [adverb] depending on [variable]
patterns.explanation: 하나의 사건이 조건에 따라 다른 양상으로 전개됨을 설정하는 기사 구조 전환 패턴
examples: Yet the effects of the rate hike are playing out unevenly depending on which sector you examine.
Yet the impact of the policy is playing out very differently depending on income level.

drillQuestions.question: This single ______ shutting down has had different consequences across the Atlantic.

sentences.notes: 기사 전체의 논지를 설정하는 thesis statement. chokepoint(병목 지점)이라는 군사/지정학 용어가 핵심이며, you stand on의 2인칭 사용이 독자를 직접 끌어들인다.

koreanAlignment.en: the consequences of this single chokepoint shutting down
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 단일 병목 지점의 폐쇄가 가져온 결과

koreanAlignment.en: depending on which side of the Atlantic

sentences.original: In the United States, the shale revolution of the past decade has transformed the country into a net energy exporter, a dramatic reversal from its import-dependent past.
sentences.translation: 미국에서는 지난 10년간의 셰일 혁명이 이 나라를 에너지 순수출국으로 변모시켰으며, 이는 수입 의존적이었던 과거로부터의 극적인 반전이다.

grammarElements.text: the shale revolution of the past decade
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: a dramatic reversal from its import-dependent past
grammarElements.label: 동격 명사구

vocabulary.example: The shale boom has reshaped global energy markets over the past decade.
The country became a net exporter of natural gas for the first time.

expressions.usage: 과거 상태와 현재의 극적 변화를 동격으로 강조하는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Event] has transformed [entity] into [new status], a dramatic reversal from [old status]
patterns.explanation: 변화의 주체, 결과, 그리고 과거와의 대비를 한 문장에 담는 구조
examples: Digitization has transformed the company into a tech leader, a dramatic reversal from its analog-era struggles.
The reform has transformed the sector into a competitive market, a dramatic reversal from decades of state monopoly.

drillQuestions.question: The shale revolution has ______ the country into a net energy exporter.

sentences.notes: 두 번째 문단의 도입문으로 미국의 구조적 강점을 소개한다. 동격 명사구(a dramatic reversal)가 변화의 규모를 한마디로 요약.

koreanAlignment.en: the shale revolution of the past decade
a dramatic reversal from its import-dependent past

sentences.original: This structural shift provides a genuine cushion, but it is far from a complete shield.
sentences.translation: 이 구조적 전환은 진정한 완충 역할을 하지만, 완전한 방패와는 거리가 멀다.

grammarElements.text: but it is far from a complete shield
grammarElements.label: but 대조절

vocabulary.example: Savings provide a financial cushion against unexpected expenses.
No trade policy is a perfect shield against global downturns.

expressions.usage: 기대와 현실의 격차를 강조할 때 사용하는 부정 강조 표현

patterns.pattern: [X] provides a genuine [A], but it is far from a complete [B]
patterns.explanation: 부분적 이점은 인정하되 충분하지 않음을 은유적 대비로 표현하는 양보-제한 구조
examples: Vaccination provides a genuine defense, but it is far from a complete guarantee.
Diversification provides a genuine buffer, but it is far from a complete solution.

drillQuestions.question: This structural shift provides a genuine ______, but it is far from a complete shield.

sentences.notes: cushion(완충)과 shield(방패)라는 두 은유를 대비시켜 미국의 에너지 독립이 부분적 보호에 불과함을 한 문장으로 전달한다. far from은 자주 등장하는 핵심 표현.

sentences.original: America still imports petroleum equivalent to over 40 percent of what it consumes domestically, and gasoline prices have climbed by roughly a third in just one month.
sentences.translation: 미국은 여전히 국내 소비량의 40% 이상에 해당하는 석유를 수입하고 있으며, 휘발유 가격은 불과 한 달 만에 약 3분의 1가량 올랐다.

grammarElements.text: petroleum equivalent to over 40 percent of what it consumes domestically
grammarElements.label: 관계절을 포함한 목적어

grammarElements.text: and gasoline prices have climbed by roughly a third in just one month
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (현재완료)

vocabulary.example: Petroleum remains the dominant fuel source for global transportation.
The product is manufactured domestically to reduce supply chain risks.

expressions.usage: 가격 변동 폭을 분수로 표현하여 체감 충격을 전달하는 저널리즘 기법

patterns.pattern: [Country] still [verbs] [quantity] of what it [consumes/produces], and [metric] has [changed] by [fraction] in [time]
patterns.explanation: 독립 주장에 대한 반증 데이터를 제시하고 시간 내 변화량을 분수로 구체화하는 데이터 중심 패턴
examples: Japan still imports ninety percent of what it needs in energy, and electricity costs have risen by a quarter in six months.
Britain still relies on imports for half of what it eats, and food prices have climbed by a fifth in one year.

drillQuestions.question: Gasoline prices have climbed by roughly a ______ in just one month.

sentences.notes: still이 앞 문장의 '순수출국' 주장에 대한 반론을 시작한다. equivalent to와 what절 구조, 분수 표현(a third)이 핵심 학습 포인트.

sentences.original: The economic pain falls unevenly: low-income households spend nearly double the share of their budgets on fuel and electricity compared to wealthy ones.
sentences.translation: 경제적 고통은 불균등하게 떨어진다: 저소득 가구는 부유한 가구에 비해 연료와 전기에 가계 예산의 거의 두 배에 달하는 비중을 쓴다.

grammarElements.text: spend nearly double the share of their budgets
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 배수 표현 목적어

grammarElements.text: on fuel and electricity compared to wealthy ones
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 + 비교 표현

vocabulary.example: The benefits of globalization have been distributed unevenly across regions.
Low-income families are disproportionately affected by rising food prices.

expressions.usage: 비율의 배수 차이를 정량적으로 강조하여 불평등을 드러내는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Pain/cost] falls unevenly: [disadvantaged group] [verb] [multiplier] compared to [advantaged group]
patterns.explanation: 콜론 뒤에 구체적 데이터를 제시하여 불균등의 실태를 증명하는 저널리즘 패턴
examples: The tax burden falls unevenly: middle-class families pay nearly triple the effective rate compared to the ultra-wealthy.
The health impact falls unevenly: rural patients wait nearly double the time compared to urban residents.

drillQuestions.question: The economic pain falls ______: low-income households spend nearly double the share.

sentences.notes: 콜론(:)이 일반적 진술과 구체적 근거를 연결하는 핵심 문장부호 역할을 한다. double the share라는 배수 표현에 주목.

sentences.original: Meanwhile, energy companies and oil-producing states such as Texas are reaping windfall profits.
sentences.translation: 한편, 에너지 기업들과 텍사스 같은 산유 주(州)들은 뜻밖의 폭리를 거두고 있다.

grammarElements.text: energy companies and oil-producing states such as Texas
grammarElements.label: 병렬 주어

vocabulary.example: Tech firms are reaping the benefits of years of investment in AI.
Oil companies reported windfall profits as crude prices doubled.

expressions.usage: 구체적 예시를 제시할 때 사용하는 표준 학술/저널리즘 표현

patterns.pattern: Meanwhile, [beneficiaries] are reaping [windfall/gains]
patterns.explanation: 앞서 언급한 피해자와 대조되는 수혜자를 Meanwhile로 전환하여 제시하는 구조
examples: Meanwhile, defense contractors are reaping record revenues.
Meanwhile, landlords are reaping higher rents from the housing shortage.

drillQuestions.question: Energy companies are ______ windfall profits.

sentences.notes: Meanwhile가 저소득 가구의 고통에서 에너지 기업의 폭리로 시선을 전환하여 대조 효과를 극대화한다. reap은 '씨를 뿌리지 않고 거둔다'는 뉘앙스.

sentences.original: The result is a stark internal redistribution of wealth flowing from struggling consumers to shareholders, deepening an inequality that was already politically toxic before the crisis began.
sentences.translation: 그 결과 허덕이는 소비자들에게서 주주들에게로 부가 흘러가는 극명한 내부적 부의 재분배가 일어나고 있으며, 위기가 시작되기 전부터 이미 정치적으로 독성이 있었던 불평등을 심화시키고 있다.

grammarElements.text: a stark internal redistribution of wealth
grammarElements.label: 보어

grammarElements.text: flowing from struggling consumers to shareholders
grammarElements.label: 현재분사 수식구

grammarElements.text: deepening an inequality that was already politically toxic before the crisis began
grammarElements.label: 결과 분사구문 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: Progressive taxation is a tool for the redistribution of wealth.
The proposal was considered politically toxic and was quickly shelved.

expressions.usage: 부의 이동 방향을 물의 흐름에 비유하는 경제 용어

patterns.pattern: The result is a [adjective] [process] flowing from [losers] to [winners], deepening [pre-existing problem]
patterns.explanation: 위기의 결과를 부의 이동 방향과 기존 문제 악화로 요약하는 분석적 결론 패턴
examples: The result is a massive transfer of value flowing from small businesses to platforms, deepening a concentration that was already alarming.
The result is a quiet subsidy flowing from savers to borrowers, deepening a generational divide that was already widening.

drillQuestions.question: The result is a stark internal ______ of wealth.

sentences.notes: 문단의 종결부로, 앞선 모든 데이터를 하나의 분석적 결론으로 수렴시킨다. politically toxic이라는 표현이 경제 문제를 정치적 프레임으로 전환.

koreanAlignment.en: a stark internal redistribution of wealth
flowing from struggling consumers to shareholders

sentences.original: Europe faces an even harsher reckoning.
sentences.translation: 유럽은 더욱 가혹한 심판에 직면해 있다.

vocabulary.example: The financial reckoning from years of excessive borrowing finally arrived.

expressions.usage: 비교급 앞에 even을 사용하여 미국보다 더 심각함을 강조하는 구조

patterns.pattern: [Entity] faces an even [comparative adjective] [challenge]
patterns.explanation: 앞서 제시된 사례보다 더 심각한 상황에 직면한 주체를 간결하게 도입하는 문단 전환 패턴
examples: Developing nations face an even steeper challenge.
The healthcare system faces an even deeper crisis.

drillQuestions.question: Europe faces an even harsher ______.

sentences.notes: 극도로 짧은 문단 전환 문장. even이 미국과의 비교를 암시하며, reckoning이라는 무거운 단어가 유럽의 상황이 더 심각함을 예고한다.

sentences.original: Natural gas prices on the continent have climbed back above levels that recall the turmoil following the 2022 disruption of Russian supplies, and they remain roughly five times higher than in the United States.
sentences.translation: 유럽 대륙의 천연가스 가격은 2022년 러시아 공급 중단 이후의 혼란을 떠올리게 하는 수준 이상으로 다시 올랐으며, 미국보다 약 5배 높은 수준을 유지하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: Natural gas prices on the continent
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: levels that recall the turmoil following the 2022 disruption of Russian supplies
grammarElements.label: 관계절을 포함한 목적어

grammarElements.text: and they remain roughly five times higher than in the United States
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (비교)

vocabulary.example: The region was plunged into economic turmoil after the currency collapsed.
The current crisis recalls the financial panic of 2008.

expressions.usage: 배수 비교로 두 지역의 극적 차이를 부각하는 정량 표현

patterns.pattern: [Metric] has climbed back above levels that recall [past crisis], and [it] remains [multiplier] higher than [comparator]
patterns.explanation: 과거 위기와의 비교 + 지역 간 배수 차이를 결합하여 이중 맥락을 제공하는 구조
examples: Inflation has climbed back above levels that recall the 1970s oil shock, and it remains roughly double the central bank's target.
Unemployment has climbed back above levels that recall the 2009 recession, and it remains three times higher than the national average.

drillQuestions.question: Gas prices recall the ______ following the 2022 disruption of Russian supplies.

sentences.notes: recall이 의인화적으로 사용되어 가격 수준 자체가 '과거를 떠올리게 한다'는 수사적 효과를 만든다. five times higher라는 배수 비교가 대서양 양안의 격차를 극적으로 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: Natural gas prices on the continent
five times higher than in the United States

sentences.original: For energy-intensive industries like chemicals and metals manufacturing, which form a significant part of the European economy, these costs are existential.
sentences.translation: 유럽 경제의 상당 부분을 차지하는 화학 및 금속 제조업 같은 에너지 집약적 산업에게 이러한 비용은 존폐가 걸린 문제이다.

grammarElements.text: For energy-intensive industries like chemicals and metals manufacturing
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (대상)

grammarElements.text: which form a significant part of the European economy
grammarElements.label: 비제한 관계절 (삽입)

vocabulary.example: Energy-intensive sectors are the first to suffer when fuel prices spike.
Climate change poses an existential threat to low-lying island nations.

expressions.usage: 단순한 경제적 부담이 아니라 생존 자체를 위협함을 한 단어로 압축하는 강력한 표현

patterns.pattern: For [affected group], which [contextual fact], [subject] is/are [dramatic adjective]
patterns.explanation: 영향을 받는 대상을 먼저 제시하고, 삽입 관계절로 그 중요성을 부연한 뒤, 강렬한 형용사로 마무리하는 구조
examples: For coastal cities, which house a third of the world's population, rising sea levels are catastrophic.
For small farmers, who produce most of the country's food, these regulations are devastating.

drillQuestions.question: For energy-intensive industries, these costs are ______.

sentences.notes: existential이 문장 끝에 단독으로 놓여 강렬한 여운을 남긴다. 비제한 관계절(which form...)이 이 산업들이 왜 중요한지를 독자에게 설명해주는 역할.

koreanAlignment.en: which form a significant part of the European economy

sentences.original: In Germany's basic chemicals sector, energy already accounts for more than 40 percent of value added, leaving little margin to absorb further price spikes.
sentences.translation: 독일의 기초 화학 부문에서 에너지는 이미 부가가치의 40% 이상을 차지하고 있어, 추가 가격 급등을 흡수할 여지가 거의 없다.

grammarElements.text: more than 40 percent of value added
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: leaving little margin to absorb further price spikes
grammarElements.label: 결과 분사구문

vocabulary.example: Tourism accounts for nearly a quarter of the country's GDP.
Manufacturing contributes the largest share of value added in the industrial sector.
Profit margins have been squeezed by rising input costs.

expressions.usage: 분사구문으로 결과를 연결하여 상황의 심각성을 부각하는 표현

patterns.pattern: In [specific sector], [cost] already accounts for [percentage] of [total], leaving little margin to [verb]
patterns.explanation: 특정 분야에서 비용 비중이 이미 높아 추가 충격을 견딜 여력이 없음을 데이터로 증명하는 패턴
examples: In the airline industry, fuel already accounts for thirty percent of operating costs, leaving little margin to absorb further increases.
In the restaurant sector, labor already accounts for forty percent of revenue, leaving little margin to raise wages further.

drillQuestions.question: Energy already ______ for more than 40 percent of value added.

sentences.notes: account for는 '차지하다'와 '설명하다' 두 가지 의미가 있으며, 여기서는 '비율을 차지하다'로 사용되었다. already가 위기 이전부터 취약했음을 강조.

koreanAlignment.en: more than 40 percent of value added

sentences.original: Governments across the continent are scrambling with familiar tools: limiting how often fuel stations can raise prices, freezing costs at the pump, and debating windfall taxes.
sentences.translation: 유럽 대륙 전역의 정부들은 익숙한 도구들을 가지고 허둥대고 있다: 주유소의 가격 인상 빈도를 제한하고, 주유소에서의 비용을 동결하며, 횡재세를 논의하는 것이다.

vocabulary.example: Regulators are scrambling to contain the fallout from the bank collapse.
Several EU countries have imposed windfall taxes on energy companies.
The government announced it would be freezing energy prices until spring.

expressions.usage: 소비자가 직접 체감하는 연료 가격을 가리키는 관용 표현

patterns.pattern: [Governments] are scrambling with [familiar/inadequate tools]: [measure 1], [measure 2], and [measure 3]
patterns.explanation: 정부가 위기에 대응하되 수단이 충분하지 않음을 암시하며 구체적 조치를 삼중 병렬로 열거하는 구조
examples: Central banks are scrambling with familiar tools: cutting rates, buying bonds, and issuing forward guidance.
School districts are scrambling with limited resources: hiring substitutes, merging classrooms, and shortening the school week.

drillQuestions.question: Governments are ______ with familiar tools to address the crisis.

sentences.notes: familiar tools라는 표현에 '이미 써본 것이라 효과가 제한적'이라는 냉소적 뉘앙스가 담겨 있다. 콜론 뒤 삼중 병렬이 정책 수단을 체계적으로 나열.

koreanAlignment.en: limiting how often fuel stations can raise prices

sentences.original: The deeper European challenge, however, is structural.
sentences.translation: 그러나 유럽의 더 깊은 과제는 구조적인 것이다.

vocabulary.example: The unemployment problem is structural, not cyclical.

expressions.usage: 표면적 문제 너머의 근본적 문제로 독자의 시선을 이끄는 전환 표현

patterns.pattern: The deeper [noun], however, is [adjective]
patterns.explanation: however 삽입으로 앞선 논의를 넘어 더 근본적인 문제를 선언하는 문단 전환 패턴
examples: The deeper problem, however, is political.
The deeper risk, however, is systemic.

drillQuestions.question: The deeper European challenge, however, is ______.

sentences.notes: 짧고 선언적인 문단 도입문. however가 콤마로 둘러싸여 삽입됨으로써 앞 문단의 '단기 대응'에서 '구조적 문제'로의 논점 전환을 명확히 한다.

sentences.original: The continent is in the middle of an enormously expensive transition from fossil fuels to renewables, requiring an estimated 1.4 trillion euros in grid investment by 2040.
sentences.translation: 유럽 대륙은 화석 연료에서 재생 에너지로의 막대한 비용이 드는 전환 한가운데에 있으며, 2040년까지 전력망 투자에 약 1조 4천억 유로가 필요하다.

grammarElements.text: in the middle of an enormously expensive transition
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 보어

grammarElements.text: requiring an estimated 1.4 trillion euros in grid investment by 2040
grammarElements.label: 결과 분사구문

vocabulary.example: The energy transition will take decades and trillions of dollars.
Investment in renewables has outpaced fossil fuels for the first time.
Upgrading the aging power grid is essential for supporting electric vehicles.

expressions.usage: 진행 중인 과정의 중간 단계에 있음을 나타내는 표현으로, 완료까지 아직 멀었음을 암시

patterns.pattern: [Entity] is in the middle of [expensive/difficult process], requiring [estimated cost] by [deadline]
patterns.explanation: 진행 중인 대규모 과정을 소개하고 그 비용/규모를 분사구문으로 구체화하는 구조
examples: The country is in the middle of a massive infrastructure overhaul, requiring an estimated five hundred billion dollars by 2035.
The industry is in the middle of a painful restructuring, requiring the elimination of two hundred thousand jobs by the end of the decade.

drillQuestions.question: The continent is in the middle of a transition from fossil fuels to ______.

sentences.notes: enormously expensive라는 부사+형용사 조합이 전환의 재정적 부담을 강조한다. 1.4 trillion euros라는 구체적 수치가 추상적 주장에 무게를 더한다.

koreanAlignment.en: in the middle of an enormously expensive transition

sentences.original: Countries with existing advantages in nuclear or hydroelectric power resist sharing infrastructure with neighbors still dependent on gas.
sentences.translation: 원자력이나 수력 발전에서 기존 이점을 가진 국가들은 여전히 가스에 의존하는 이웃 국가들과 인프라를 공유하는 것을 거부한다.

grammarElements.text: Countries with existing advantages in nuclear or hydroelectric power
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 수식을 포함한 주어

grammarElements.text: sharing infrastructure with neighbors still dependent on gas
grammarElements.label: 동명사 목적어

vocabulary.example: Norway generates nearly all its electricity from hydroelectric sources.
Several member states resisted the proposal to share defense costs.

expressions.usage: 다른 국가들이 전환을 완료하지 못한 상태를 강조하는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Advantaged group] resist [verb-ing] [resource] with [disadvantaged group] still [adjective]
patterns.explanation: 이미 이점을 가진 측이 아직 취약한 측과 자원을 나누기를 거부하는 갈등 구조를 표현하는 패턴
examples: Wealthy regions resist sharing tax revenue with provinces still struggling with poverty.
Advanced economies resist opening markets to countries still developing their industries.

drillQuestions.question: Countries with advantages in nuclear power ______ sharing infrastructure.

sentences.notes: resist + 동명사(sharing) 구조에 주목. resist는 to부정사가 아닌 동명사를 목적어로 취한다. still dependent가 형용사구로 neighbors를 후치 수식.

koreanAlignment.en: Countries with existing advantages in nuclear or hydroelectric power
koreanAlignment.ko: 원자력이나 수력 발전에서 기존 이점을 가진 국가들

sentences.original: The carbon pricing system that underpins climate policy is under political attack from leaders desperate to lower industrial costs.
sentences.translation: 기후 정책을 뒷받침하는 탄소 가격 제도가 산업 비용을 낮추려고 안달인 지도자들의 정치적 공격을 받고 있다.

grammarElements.text: from leaders desperate to lower industrial costs
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 + 형용사 후치 수식

vocabulary.example: Consumer spending underpins much of the country's economic growth.
Companies are desperate to find workers amid the labor shortage.

expressions.usage: 정책이나 제도가 정치적 반대에 직면해 있음을 나타내는 표현

patterns.pattern: The [system] that underpins [policy] is under [type of] attack from [actors] desperate to [goal]
patterns.explanation: 핵심 제도가 위기 상황에서 단기적 이해관계에 의해 위협받는 구조를 표현하는 패턴
examples: The regulatory framework that underpins financial stability is under lobbying attack from banks desperate to ease capital requirements.
The welfare system that underpins social cohesion is under fiscal attack from governments desperate to cut spending.

drillQuestions.question: The carbon pricing system that ______ climate policy is under attack.

sentences.notes: underpins와 under attack의 under 반복이 미묘한 수사적 효과를 만든다. desperate가 leaders를 후치 수식하는 구조에 주목.

koreanAlignment.en: leaders desperate to lower industrial costs

sentences.original: Every stakeholder, from consumers to corporations to national governments, is trying to push the burden of this transformation onto someone else.
sentences.translation: 소비자에서 기업, 국가 정부에 이르기까지 모든 이해관계자가 이 변혁의 부담을 다른 누군가에게 떠넘기려 하고 있다.

grammarElements.text: from consumers to corporations to national governments
grammarElements.label: 삽입 열거구 (from A to B to C)

vocabulary.example: All stakeholders must be consulted before the policy is finalized.
The tax burden falls disproportionately on the middle class.

expressions.usage: 책임이나 비용을 다른 주체에게 전가하려는 행위를 표현하는 구동사

patterns.pattern: Every [actor], from [A] to [B] to [C], is trying to push [cost/burden] onto someone else
patterns.explanation: 모든 이해관계자를 열거하며 각자 부담을 전가하려는 집단적 회피 행태를 표현하는 구조
examples: Every party, from labor unions to employers to the treasury, is trying to push the adjustment costs onto someone else.
Every country, from the US to China to Europe, is trying to push the responsibility for emissions onto someone else.

drillQuestions.question: Every stakeholder is trying to push the ______ onto someone else.

sentences.notes: from A to B to C의 삼중 열거가 문제의 규모를 보여주며, someone else로 끝나는 것이 아무도 책임을 지려 하지 않는 상황을 풍자적으로 마무리한다.

koreanAlignment.en: from consumers to corporations to national governments
push the burden of this transformation

sentences.original: What unites both sides of the Atlantic is a sobering lesson: even net energy exporters cannot fully escape a global price shock, and no amount of domestic production eliminates vulnerability to disruptions in a deeply interconnected market.
sentences.translation: 대서양 양안을 하나로 묶는 것은 냉엄한 교훈이다: 에너지 순수출국조차 글로벌 가격 충격을 완전히 피할 수 없으며, 아무리 많은 국내 생산으로도 깊이 상호 연결된 시장에서의 교란에 대한 취약성을 제거할 수 없다.

grammarElements.text: What unites both sides of the Atlantic
grammarElements.label: what 명사절 주어

grammarElements.text: even net energy exporters cannot fully escape a global price shock
grammarElements.label: 콜론 이후 부연절 1

grammarElements.text: no amount of domestic production eliminates vulnerability to disruptions
grammarElements.label: 등위절 2 (절대 부정)

vocabulary.example: The report offered a sobering assessment of the climate crisis.
The cyberattack exposed critical vulnerabilities in the banking system.
Global supply chains are deeply interconnected, making local disruptions a worldwide concern.

expressions.usage: 절대적 불가능성을 강조하는 부정 표현으로, 해결책의 한계를 단언할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: What unites [opposing parties] is [lesson]: even [X] cannot [verb], and no amount of [Y] eliminates [Z]
patterns.explanation: 대립하는 양측의 공통 교훈을 제시하고 콜론 뒤에 이중 부정으로 그 교훈을 구체화하는 결론적 구조
examples: What unites both parties is a harsh truth: even booming economies cannot outgrow debt, and no amount of monetary easing eliminates structural imbalances.
What unites rich and poor nations is a shared risk: even the wealthiest cannot buy immunity, and no amount of preparation eliminates exposure to pandemics.

drillQuestions.question: What unites both sides of the Atlantic is a ______ lesson.

sentences.notes: 마지막 문단의 핵심 문장으로, 기사 전체의 논지를 수렴한다. What절 주어와 콜론 이후의 이중 부정(even ... cannot, no amount of ... eliminates)이 교훈의 절대성을 강조.

koreanAlignment.en: What unites both sides of the Atlantic
even net energy exporters cannot fully escape
koreanAlignment.ko: 에너지 순수출국조차 완전히 피할 수 없다

koreanAlignment.en: no amount of domestic production eliminates vulnerability
koreanAlignment.ko: 아무리 많은 국내 생산으로도 취약성을 제거할 수 없다

sentences.original: The crisis has exposed how fragile energy security remains and how unevenly the costs of that fragility are distributed within societies.
sentences.translation: 이 위기는 에너지 안보가 여전히 얼마나 취약한지, 그리고 그 취약성의 비용이 사회 내에서 얼마나 불균등하게 분배되는지를 드러냈다.

grammarElements.text: and how unevenly the costs of that fragility are distributed within societies
grammarElements.label: 간접의문절 목적어 2

vocabulary.example: The pandemic exposed deep inequalities in access to healthcare.
The ceasefire was fragile and could collapse at any moment.
The fragility of global supply chains became apparent during the crisis.

expressions.usage: 간접의문문에서 형용사를 how 뒤에 배치하여 정도를 강조하는 구문

patterns.pattern: [Crisis] has exposed how [adjective] [X] remains and how [adverb] [Y] is [distributed/affected]
patterns.explanation: 위기가 두 가지 진실을 동시에 드러냈음을 병렬 간접의문절로 표현하는 분석적 결론 구조
examples: The recession has exposed how vulnerable workers remain and how unequally recovery benefits are shared.
The scandal has exposed how weak oversight remains and how easily regulations are circumvented.

drillQuestions.question: The crisis has ______ how fragile energy security remains.

sentences.notes: fragile과 fragility의 어근 반복이 '취약함'이라는 주제를 이중으로 강조한다. 두 개의 how절이 병렬되어 위기가 드러낸 두 가지 진실을 체계적으로 정리.

koreanAlignment.en: how unevenly the costs are distributed

sentences.original: For policymakers everywhere, the question is no longer whether to diversify energy sources but how quickly they can do so before the next disruption arrives.
sentences.translation: 전 세계 정책 입안자들에게 문제는 더 이상 에너지원을 다각화할지 여부가 아니라, 다음 교란이 닥치기 전에 얼마나 빨리 그렇게 할 수 있느냐이다.

grammarElements.text: no longer whether to diversify energy sources
grammarElements.label: 보어 1 (부정)

grammarElements.text: but how quickly they can do so before the next disruption arrives
grammarElements.label: 보어 2 (대조)

vocabulary.example: The fund manager advised clients to diversify their portfolios across asset classes.
Policymakers are debating new regulations for the technology sector.

expressions.phrase: no longer whether ... but how quickly
expressions.meaning: 더 이상 ~할지 여부가 아니라 얼마나 빨리
expressions.usage: 논쟁의 단계가 이미 넘어갔음을 선언하며 긴급성을 강조하는 수사적 대조 표현

patterns.pattern: The question is no longer whether to [verb] but how [quickly/effectively] [subject] can do so before [deadline/threat]
patterns.explanation: 논쟁의 초점이 '할지 말지'에서 '얼마나 빨리'로 이동했음을 선언하는 기사 마무리 패턴
examples: The question is no longer whether to regulate AI but how effectively governments can do so before the technology outpaces oversight.
The question is no longer whether to cut emissions but how dramatically nations can do so before critical thresholds are crossed.

drillQuestions.question: The question is no longer whether to ______ energy sources but how quickly.

sentences.notes: 기사의 마지막 문장으로, no longer whether ... but how로 논쟁의 단계가 이미 넘어갔음을 선언한다. before the next disruption arrives가 긴박감을 더하며 독자에게 행동의 시급성을 남긴다.

koreanAlignment.en: the question is no longer whether to diversify
koreanAlignment.ko: 문제는 더 이상 다각화할지 여부가 아니라

koreanAlignment.en: but how quickly they can do so
koreanAlignment.ko: 얼마나 빨리 그렇게 할 수 있느냐
